era_context: Continuation of the Middle Bronze Age in the Levant and Egypt; the reign of Amenemhat III in Egypt (or the final years of his reign, depending on chronological system). In Mesopotamia, the post-Hammurabi period of fragmentation. The Hittite Old Kingdom is consolidating in Anatolia. The Indus Valley Civilization remains in its mature phase. In China, the traditional Xia Dynasty continues (though archaeological evidence is contested).

events.title: Continuation of Amenemhat III's reign and building programs
events.description: Amenemhat III continues his extensive building and administrative programs. This year falls within the period of his documented activity, though no specific events are recorded for this particular year. His reign is characterized by the construction of temples, fortifications, and the famous Labyrinth at Hawara. The period also sees the continuation of trade with Nubia, the Levant, and the Aegean.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from temple and palace sites
Inscriptions and reliefs from Amenemhat III's building projects

events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for this year. The dating is conventional, based on the estimated length of Amenemhat III's reign. Uncertainty is ±20–30 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are attested for this year. The broader claim that Amenemhat III's reign was a period of uninterrupted prosperity is complicated by evidence of environmental stress (low Nile floods) and administrative challenges in his later years.
historiographic_note: This year is not specifically documented in surviving sources. The historical record for this period is reconstructed from the broader context of Amenemhat III's reign and the Middle Kingdom period. The absence of year-specific records is typical for ancient Egypt; most surviving texts are either administrative documents (which are rarely dated to specific years) or commemorative inscriptions (which record major events, not routine activities). The dating of this year depends entirely on the chronological framework applied to Amenemhat III's reign, which is itself uncertain by ±20–30 years.

graph_edges.note: This year is part of the same reign as -1820-001.
